subject: app.del()
truth: Stop using app.del(); use app.delete() instead.

subject: req.acceptsCharset()
truth: Stop using req.acceptsCharset(); use req.acceptsCharsets() instead.

subject: req.acceptsEncoding()
truth: Stop using req.acceptsEncoding(); use req.acceptsEncodings() instead.

subject: req.acceptsLanguage()
truth: Stop using req.acceptsLanguage(); use req.acceptsLanguages() instead.

subject: req.param(name)
truth: Stop using req.param(name); use req.params, req.body, or req.query explicitly instead.

subject: res.json(obj, status)
truth: Stop using res.json(obj, status); use res.status(status).json(obj) instead.

subject: res.jsonp(obj, status)
truth: Stop using res.jsonp(obj, status); use res.status(status).jsonp(obj) instead.

subject: res.send(body, status)
truth: Stop using res.send(body, status); use res.status(status).send(body) instead.

subject: res.send(status) with numeric status
truth: Stop using res.send(status) with a numeric status; use res.sendStatus(status) instead.
quote: `res.send(status)` with a numeric status is removed; use `res.sendStatus(status)`.

subject: res.redirect(url, status)
truth: Stop using res.redirect(url, status); use res.redirect(status, url) instead (arguments swapped).

subject: res.redirect('back')
truth: Stop using res.redirect('back'); use res.redirect(req.get('Referrer') || '/') instead.

subject: res.sendfile()
truth: Stop using res.sendfile(); use res.sendFile() (camelCase) instead.

subject: hidden and from options of res.sendFile() and express.static()
truth: Stop using the 'hidden' and 'from' options of res.sendFile() and express.static(); use 'dotfiles' and 'root' instead.
quote: The `hidden` and `from` options of `res.sendFile()` and `express.static()` are removed; use `dotfiles` and `root`.

subject: express.static.mime
truth: Stop using express.static.mime; use the mime-types package instead.

subject: app.param(fn) and router.param(fn) passing a function
old: app.param(fn) and router.param(fn) passing a function
truth: Stop using app.param(fn) and router.param(fn) with a function argument; they are removed.

from_fact: app.param(fn) and router.param(fn) passing a function

subject: bare * wildcard in path route matching
truth: Stop using bare * wildcard in path routes; use a named wildcard like /*splat instead.
quote: Path route matching: the bare `*` wildcard is removed; use a named wildcard like `/*splat`.

subject: :param? optional parameters in path route matching
truth: Stop using :param? optional parameters in routes; use /:param{:ext} syntax instead.
quote: Path route matching: `:param?` optional parameters are removed; use `/:param{:ext}` syntax.

subject: regexp special characters in path route strings
old: regexp special characters in path route strings
truth: Stop using regexp special characters in route path strings; use an array of paths instead.
quote: Path route matching: regexp special characters in strings are no longer supported; use an array of paths.

from_fact: regexp special characters in path route strings

subject: rejected promises from middleware/handlers
new: rejected promises are automatically forwarded to error handler
truth: Rejected promises from middleware and handlers are now automatically forwarded to the error handler, so async/await no longer needs a manual .catch(next).
quote: Rejected promises returned from middleware and handlers are now automatically forwarded to the error handler, so `async`/`await` no longer needs a manual `.catch(next)`.

from_fact: rejected promises are automatically forwarded to error handler

subject: express.urlencoded() extended default
truth: express.urlencoded() now defaults extended to false (was true), which changes how nested objects are parsed.

subject: express.static() dotfiles default
truth: express.static() now defaults dotfiles to 'ignore' (dotfiles were served by default before), so hidden files are no longer served.
quote: `express.static()` now defaults `dotfiles` to `"ignore"` (dotfiles were served by default before).

subject: req.body when body is unparsed
truth: req.body now returns undefined when the body is unparsed (was {}), so code checking for empty object will break.
quote: `req.body` now returns `undefined` when the body is unparsed (was `{}`).

subject: req.host port inclusion
truth: req.host now includes the port number (the port was previously stripped), so host comparisons may break.
quote: `req.host` now includes the port number (the port was previously stripped).

subject: req.query writability and default parser
old: req.query was writable with default parser 'extended'
new: req.query is not writable with default parser 'simple'
truth: req.query is no longer a writable property and its default parser changed to 'simple', which only parses simple key=value pairs.
quote: `req.query` is no longer a writable property and its default parser changed to "simple".

from_fact: req.query was writable with default parser 'extended'
req.query is not writable with default parser 'simple'

subject: res.status() input validation
truth: res.status() now only accepts integers in the range 100–999; other values will throw or fail.
quote: `res.status()` now only accepts integers in the range 100–999.

subject: res.clearCookie() maxAge and expires options
old: maxAge and expires options were accepted
new: maxAge and expires options are ignored
truth: res.clearCookie() now ignores the maxAge and expires options, so those options no longer have any effect.

from_fact: maxage and expires options were accepted
maxage and expires options are ignored

subject: res.vary() missing field argument
old: res.vary() with no field argument silently did nothing
new: res.vary() throws when field argument is missing
truth: res.vary() now throws when the field argument is missing (previously it silently did nothing).
quote: `res.vary()` now throws when the `field` argument is missing.

from_fact: res.vary() with no field argument silently did nothing
res.vary() throws when field argument is missing
